title: scirpus holoschoenus var. australis
intro: Scirpus holoschoenus var. australis, also known as the yellow sedge or coastal club-rush, is a type of sedge plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and aiding in the treatment of fever and skin conditions. Scirpus holoschoenus var. australis can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusion, decoction, or poultice applications.
